title: Accessible Dwelling Unit with 36-inch Route Through the Unit (HUD)
creator: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development, Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity
rightsholder_or_source: HUD, Fair Housing Act Design Manual (1998), Chapter 4 'Accessible Route Into and Through the Covered Dwelling Unit', page 4.3
research_notes: Rendered and cropped from the public-domain HUD Fair Housing Act Design Manual, Chapter 4 PDF (page 4.3), figure captioned '36-Inch Wide Minimum Accessible Route Through Dwelling Unit'. This is the whole-unit accessible plan that complements the room-scale turning-circle figure in plan 36.
research_features: A complete one-bedroom apartment showing the shaded 36-inch accessible route threading from the entrance through the living room, kitchen, bedroom, and two bathrooms, adjoining the clear floor space at every appliance and fixture, plus a wood deck reached with a minimal level change.
feature_highlights: a complete one-bedroom apartment unit, not just a single room
a shaded 36-inch minimum accessible route threading from the entrance through every room
the route adjoins clear floor space at all kitchen appliances and all bathroom fixtures
two bathrooms, both required to have usable doors
a wood deck reached with a 1/2 inch maximum change in level
callouts annotating the route, the clearances, and the door requirements
furniture and fixtures drawn in to show the clearances are real
supported_examples: the straight-walled, right-angled rooms and the overall unit footprint
room layout with doors and door swings
roadmap_examples: furniture and fixtures (sofa, bed, kitchen appliances, bathroom fixtures) as placed assets

gap_features.why: the entire unit is organized around an accessible route of a minimum width that must adjoin clear floor space at every appliance and fixture, with turning space in the kitchen and baths; the editor cannot draw a route, reserve clear floor space, or verify the route reaches every fixture
the wood deck is an exterior space attached to the unit and joined to it by the accessible route, not an enclosed interior room
